data.body: Reclassified intent-source-fidelity in epic 6f2d per user's critical challenge (accepted): it is NOT an agent/tool-using criterion. Assessing intent alignment is a text-vs-text comparison of artifacts we already hold (linked session log + pre-resolved ticket graph) FED as context to a single FRONTIER-model call (likely 2-STEP: extract expressed requirements -> check plan-graph against each, like E1/E3/E6). No filesystem/tool access. I had conflated 'needs frontier model + large context' (true) with 'needs the agent loop' (false). Only honest pro-agent argument = scale/retrieval over very large or multiple intent sources; resolved by DETERMINISTIC pre-retrieval (chunk/grep + pre-fetch graph) feeding the single call, NOT an autonomous agent loop (cheaper/faster/reproducible — matters for a gate). Agent would only be warranted for intent-vs-BUILT (code tools), a different check (G6/E4), not intent-vs-plan. Constraints retained: fires only when a session log is linked; frontier model (Opus). Epic still passes check-ac(9)+clarity(7).
